title: Jobs Filters Journey And Flow Collection

governing.notesMd: Journeys and flows define UX behavior paths before low-level requirements.

items.title: User narrows Jobs results with filters

journey.userGoal: Narrow Jobs results to a set of opportunities that match the user's intent.
journey.userContext: The user is browsing Jobs and wants more precise results than the current broad list.
journey.trigger: The user opens search or advanced filter controls.
journey.startingState: Jobs browsing is available and may already contain active filters.
journey.successOutcome: The user sees updated Jobs results that reflect the intended filter constraints.
alternatePaths: Dismiss modal and keep prior browsing context.
hesitationOrFailurePoints: The user is unsure whether edits are live or pending.
Clearing filters could remove more context than expected.
recoveryExpectations: Invalid input explains what must be fixed.
nonGoals: Define result ranking or backend query behavior.

confirmationBaseline.notesMd: Backfilled as the audit baseline for the one-pass UX spec creation/update. This records provenance only; it does not replace UX authority, product confirmation, behavior mappings, or runtime drift ledgers.

flow.purpose: Let users edit advanced filters before committing them to live Jobs browsing.
flow.startsWhen: The user opens advanced filters from Jobs search or arrives with filter-editing context.
flow.endsWhen: The user applies, cancels, discards, clears, or dismisses the filter modal.
happyPath: Modal opens with current browsing context represented.
Apply becomes available when edits can affect browsing.
Modal closes and Jobs browsing reflects the applied filters.
alternatePaths: User cancels and returns to prior browsing context.
User fixes invalid input before applying.
blockedPaths: Apply is not meaningful when no browse-impacting edits exist.
recoveryPaths: Validation guidance lets user fix blocked input.
Cancel/discard returns user to prior browsing context.
expectedOutcomes: Committed filters are visible in browsing.
Uncommitted edits are not silently applied.
nonGoals: Define how filter state is stored internally.

confirmationBaseline.notesMd: Backfilled as the audit baseline for the one-pass UX spec creation/update. This records provenance only; it does not replace UX authority, product confirmation, behavior mappings, or runtime drift ledgers.

subflow.purpose: Commit valid modal filter edits to live Jobs browsing.
subflow.trigger: The user invokes Apply while filter edits are valid and applying would affect browsing.
subflow.userIntent: Apply filter changes and return to updated results.
visibleSteps: Apply is visible when applying matters.
Apply is enabled only when the edited filters are valid.
subflow.expectedResult: Live Jobs browsing reflects the applied filters.
alternateResults: If invalid, Apply remains visible but disabled until the user fixes the problem.
If applying would not change browsing, Apply is not shown as a meaningful action.
edgeCases: User creates invalid input after Apply was previously available.
mustNotHappen: Apply must not silently save a reusable filter definition.
Apply must not close the modal without updating visible browsing when it reports success.

confirmationBaseline.notesMd: Backfilled as the audit baseline for the one-pass UX spec creation/update. This records provenance only; it does not replace UX authority, product confirmation, behavior mappings, or runtime drift ledgers.
